message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_renorm autograd via permute/reshape composition [bd-go4q]

tensor_renorm used to extract values, conditionally scale in plain
f64, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing
autograd through embedding-regularization paths
(torch.nn.Embedding(max_norm=...) is the canonical user). PyTorch's
torch.renorm IS differentiable.

Closed-form scale per slice:
   scale_d = maxnorm / max(norm_d, maxnorm)
which equals 1 when norm_d <= maxnorm and maxnorm/norm_d when
norm_d > maxnorm. clamp_min(norm, maxnorm) gives exactly the
denom we want for both cases.

Strategy:
  permute dim to 0 (no-op when dim==0)
  reshape to [dim_size, other_size]
  abs + pow(p) + sum_dim(1) + pow(1/p) → norm per slice
  denom = clamp_min(norm, maxnorm)
  scale = maxnorm / denom
  expand to [dim_size, other_size], multiply
  reshape + permute back

All ops are autograd-aware so gradients flow through both branches
(identity for unsc clipped rows, chain through scale for clipped
rows). Adds renorm_propagates_gradient_through_input regression
test.

Closes frankentorch-go4q. ft-api lib tests: 1166 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
